title: Dharmasthala Temple
tagline: Dharmasthala Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Dharmasthala Temple (Shri Kṣhētra Dharmasthala) is an 800-year-old Hindu religious institution in the temple town of Dharmasthala in Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Dharmasthala Temple (Shri Kṣhētra Dharmasthala) is an 800-year-old Hindu religious institution in the temple town of Dharmasthala in Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. The deities of the temple are Hindu god Shiva, who is referred to as Mañjunatha, Hindu goddess Ammanavaru (meaning mother), the Tirthankara Chandraprabha and the protective gods of Jainism, Kalarahu, Kalarkayi, Kumarasvami and Kanyakumari. The temple was reconsecrated in 16th century by Hindu Dvaita saint Vadiraja Tirtha by the request of the then administrator of the temple, Devaraja Heggade. The temple is considered unique, s

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Manjusha Museum
topPlaces.description: Manjusha Museum is in the town of Dharmasthala in Karnataka state, India. It houses antiques, paintings, artifacts, temple chariots, and vintage cars collected from temples across Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Dharmasthala
topPlaces.description: Dharmasthala () (earlier known as Kuduma) is an Indian temple town on the banks of the Nethravathi River in the taluk of Belthangady of the Dakshina Kannada or southern district in Karnataka, India. The town is known for its centuries-old Dharmasthala Temple, devoted to the Hindu god Manjunatha.

topPlaces.name: Kakkinje
topPlaces.description: Kakkinje is a village located in Dakshina Kannada District of Karnataka State in India and comes under Belthangady Taluk. The village incorporates an eclectic mix of religious denominations, including Christians, Hindus and Muslims.

topPlaces.name: Kalmanja village
topPlaces.description: Kalmanja is a village in the Belthangady taluk in Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. Kalmanja has around 2000 people, most of whom are farmers and agriculturists.

topPlaces.name: Tulu Nadu
topPlaces.description: Tulu Nadu, or Tulunad, is a region and a proposed state on the southwestern coast of India. The region is predominantly inhabited by the Tulu people, also known as Tuluva, who speak Tulu, a Dravidian language.

topPlaces.name: South Canara
topPlaces.description: South Canara (South Kannada) was a district of the Madras Presidency of British Raj, located at 13.00°N 75.40°E / 13.00; 75.40. It comprised the towns of Kasaragod and Udupi and adjacent villages, with the administration at Mangalore city.

topPlaces.name: Ujire
topPlaces.description: Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

topPlaces.name: St. Antony Church, Ujire
topPlaces.description: St. Antony Church is a Catholic church in Ujire, within the Belthangady Varado of the Diocese of Mangalore, India.

items.note: Manjusha Museum is in the town of Dharmasthala in Karnataka state, India. It houses antiques, paintings, artifacts, temple chariots, and vintage cars collected from temples across Karnataka.

Dharmasthala () (earlier known as Kuduma) is an Indian temple town on the banks of the Nethravathi River in the taluk of Belthangady of the Dakshina Kannada or southern district in Karnataka, India. The town is known for its centuries-old Dharmasthala Temple, devoted to the Hindu god Manjunatha.

Kakkinje is a village located in Dakshina Kannada District of Karnataka State in India and comes under Belthangady Taluk. The village incorporates an eclectic mix of religious denominations, including Christians, Hindus and Muslims.
Kalmanja is a village in the Belthangady taluk in Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. Kalmanja has around 2000 people, most of whom are farmers and agriculturists.

Tulu Nadu, or Tulunad, is a region and a proposed state on the southwestern coast of India. The region is predominantly inhabited by the Tulu people, also known as Tuluva, who speak Tulu, a Dravidian language.

South Canara (South Kannada) was a district of the Madras Presidency of British Raj, located at 13.00°N 75.40°E / 13.00; 75.40. It comprised the towns of Kasaragod and Udupi and adjacent villages, with the administration at Mangalore city.
Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.
St. Antony Church is a Catholic church in Ujire, within the Belthangady Varado of the Diocese of Mangalore, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dharmasthala Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dharmasthala Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dharmasthala Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (59 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dharmasthala Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dharmasthala Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dharmasthala Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Dharmasthala Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dharmasthala Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Manjusha Museum, Dharmasthala, Kakkinje, Kalmanja village, Tulu Nadu.
